description_long: This is a tall, intricately carved stone lantern, likely of Balinese or Southeast Asian origin, featuring a multi-tiered pagoda-like structure. The base is circular and robust, adorned with ornate reliefs and resting on a square stone platform surrounded by patches of moss and greenery. The midsection displays detailed sculpted figures, possibly mythological guardians, each facing outward from the central column. The upper portion is crowned with a lotus-shaped finial and additional decorative bands, all rendered in weathered gray stone. The lantern is heavily ornamented with swirling motifs and floral patterns, giving it a ceremonial and ancient appearance. The presence of moss and plant growth suggests it is an outdoor fixture, blending harmoniously with its natural surroundings.
description: A tall, ornately carved stone lantern with mythological figures and floral motifs, set on a mossy stone base. The structure is weathered and richly detailed, evoking a ceremonial and ancient aesthetic.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main sculpted figure and the most detailed reliefs, with moss and greenery on the left side of the base.
description_view_1: Slightly rotated, this view highlights the lantern's right side, revealing more of the moss and plant growth encroaching on the base.
description_view_2: Rear view with less visible sculpted detail, focusing on the lush greenery and the back of the stone platform.
description_view_3: Left-side view showing another guardian figure and the ornate carvings, with dense foliage on the left and rear of the base.
